top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 99, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Velothon Berlin's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
Velothon Berlin's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Velothon Berlin's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 99 miles.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 99 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Velothon Berlin. Train it until it feels boring.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 99 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
At Velothon Berlin, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Velothon Berlin's mixed terrain actually does to you.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 99 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.heat: July in Berlin, Germany can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Velothon Berlin. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Velothon Berlin’s 99 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 99 miles of mixed terrain in Berlin, Germany.
